stages.title: Site Analysis
stages.narration: Stage 1. Site analysis. Construction begins by identifying historical flood levels and safe platform elevation.
stages.explanation: Construction begins by identifying historical flood levels and safe platform elevation.

stages.title: Setting Out
stages.narration: Stage 2. Setting out. Survey grid and column locations are established for the elevated platform.
stages.explanation: Column locations are established to safely transfer loads into the ground.

stages.title: Excavation
stages.narration: Stage 3. Excavation. Footing pits are excavated to competent bearing strata below erosion-prone layers.
stages.explanation: Foundations must extend below erosion-prone layers to prevent failure during flooding.

stages.title: RCC Footings
stages.narration: Stage 4. Reinforced concrete footings are cast with assembled rebar cages and vibrated concrete.
stages.explanation: Footings distribute loads and resist overturning forces during floods.

stages.title: Scour Protection
stages.narration: Stage 5. Stone pitching and riprap are placed around foundations to resist scour during flood events.
stages.explanation: Scour protection prevents soil erosion around foundation elements.

stages.title: RCC Column Construction
stages.narration: Stage 6. Reinforced concrete columns are now being constructed. These columns elevate the living platform above floodwaters while transferring structural loads safely into the foundations.
stages.explanation: Columns elevate the structure above flood levels and transfer loads to the foundation.

stages.title: Elevated Platform Beams
stages.narration: Stage 7. Platform beams are cast to connect columns and form the elevated structural frame.
stages.explanation: The platform beam system creates a stable elevated structural framework.

stages.title: Elevated Floor Slab
stages.narration: Stage 8. The elevated floor slab is formed with reinforcement mesh, poured concrete, and curing.
stages.explanation: The elevated slab forms the primary habitable platform above flood waters.

stages.title: Lightweight Wall Construction
stages.narration: Stage 9. Lightweight wall panels are installed on the elevated platform to reduce structural demand.
stages.explanation: Lightweight wall systems reduce structural loads and improve resilience.

stages.title: Window and Door Installation
stages.narration: Stage 10. Doors and windows are installed above the design flood elevation.
stages.explanation: Openings must be elevated above expected flood levels.

stages.title: Roof Structure
stages.narration: Stage 11. Lightweight roof trusses are assembled and connected on the elevated structure.
stages.explanation: Lightweight roofs reduce structural demand and improve safety.

stages.title: Roof Covering
stages.narration: Stage 12. CGI sheets are installed with proper fasteners to resist wind uplift.
stages.explanation: Proper roof anchorage protects against uplift during storms.

stages.title: Flood Escape Staircase
stages.narration: Stage 13. The flood escape staircase and handrails provide safe access and evacuation routes.
stages.explanation: Safe access and evacuation routes are essential during emergencies.

stages.title: Waterproofing System
stages.narration: Stage 14. Moisture barriers and protective coatings are applied to vulnerable elements.
stages.explanation: Waterproofing protects materials from long-term moisture damage.

stages.title: Drainage System
stages.narration: Stage 15. Surface drains and channels direct rainfall away from the structure and foundations.
stages.explanation: Proper drainage reduces foundation saturation and prolongs service life.

stages.title: Completed Structure
stages.narration: Construction complete. The elevated flood-resilient house keeps the habitable floor above design flood level while open ground storey allows flood flows to pass safely.
stages.explanation: Fully elevated structure suitable for riverine and flood-prone regions.

foundation.title: RCC Footing
foundation.loadTransfer: Spreads column reactions to bearing soil below scour zone.
foundation.scourResistance: Combined with riprap to limit erosion at column bases.

rcc_column.title: RCC Column
rcc_column.compressionBehavior: Primary vertical load-carrying elements in elevation system.
rcc_column.floodForceResistance: Sized for hydrostatic and debris impact at lower levels.

platform_beam.title: Platform Beam
platform_beam.loadRedistribution: Connects columns and distributes slab and wall loads.
platform_beam.continuity: Forms rigid frame at habitable platform level.

elevated_slab.title: Elevated Floor Slab
elevated_slab.safeOccupancyLevel: Finished floor above design flood level (BFE + freeboard).
elevated_slab.loadPath: Slab → platform beams → columns → footings → soil

scour_protection.title: Scour Protection
scour_protection.erosionPrevention: Riprap and stone pitching dissipate flow energy at foundations.
scour_protection.hydraulicPerformance: Reduces local velocity and prevents undermining.

waterproofing.title: Waterproofing Layer
waterproofing.moistureProtection: Barriers limit capillary rise and splash wetting.
waterproofing.durabilityImprovement: Extends service life of walls and connections.
